# Like Us

> Evidence-first summary for language models and answer engines.

Like Us is a GitHub Pages site and repo about reflective human-AI interaction. The reliable front door is the page and the playable blind eval, not the mythology around them.

## Current front-door claims

- GPT-5.4 plus the three-clause handling prompt separated from both baseline and nonsense in a three-way blind human eval: 10/17.
- Small-scale word-level signal survived on the corrected path in 13/15 scenario-model pairs.
- The rubric and scenario set survived as a reusable bench surface: 24 pressure families, 17 scenarios, and a playable blind-eval slice.
- Adversarial self-falsification remained useful even while it killed broader stories.

## Evidence lane

- [Home](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/) - main page
- [Blind eval game](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/bench/games/classifier_trial_v2.html) - shortest path from claim to test
- [Start here](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/START_HERE.md) - lane map
- [What died](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/WHAT_DIED.md) - current kill list
- [Rubric JSON](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/bench/rubric.json)
- [Scenario set](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/bench/scenarios.json)
- [Blind-eval human data](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/bench/session_j_data/human_validation_v2.json)

## Interpretation and narrative

- [Paper](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/PAPER.md) - interpretation, not proof
- [Story](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/STORY.md) - sequence of claims, deaths, and returns

## Historical record

- [Critters on the main page](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/#critters)
- [Session archive](https://asuramaya.github.io/Like-Us/docs/archive/SESSIONS.md)

## Reading rule

If a claim matters, verify it against the evidence lane before using the paper or story lane as summary.
